theory.canonical_statement: At the TZP, the metric tensor satisfies $\det g|_p = 0$ and the inverse metric $g^{-1}|_p$ does not exist; for $x\neq p$, $\det g(x)\neq 0$ and $g^{-1}(x)$ exists.
theory.technical_interpretation: At the TZP, the metric tensor satisfies $\det g|_p = 0$ and the inverse metric $g^{-1}|_p$ does not exist; for $x\neq p$, $\det g(x)\neq 0$ and $g^{-1}(x)$ exists.

equation_progression.interpretive_role: This identifies the singular metric event that distinguishes the zero point from every ordinary manifold location.
This strengthens the claim from a scalar determinant statement to a genuine loss of geometric inversion at the anchor.
This closes the progression by confining the collapse to the TZP so later dynamics can still be computed on the surrounding manifold.

external_sources.title: Metric Degeneracy Condition
external_sources.canonical_claim: At the TZP, the metric tensor satisfies $\det g|_p = 0$ and the inverse metric $g^{-1}|_p$ does not exist; for $x\neq p$, $\det g(x)\neq 0$ and $g^{-1}(x)$ exists.
external_sources.exact_language: This source (Pseudoinversion of Degenerate Metrics) supports the mathematical structure (degenerate metric) by showing that The article shows that when a metric is degenerate, its inverse g* is not defined, which prevents standard definitions of gradient, divergence, and Laplacian.
external_sources.remaining_gap: While the cited source provides partial support, a dedicated mathematical or physical proof specific to the Trawinistic framework is still required to fully establish this entry.
external_sources.relevance: The article shows that when a metric is degenerate, its inverse g* is not defined, which prevents standard definitions of gradient, divergence, and Laplacian. The authors propose pseudoinverse methods to generalize differential operators. This supports the claim that at the zero point, the inverse metric does not exist while away from the zero point the metric is non‑degenerate.

promotion_ledger.note: Append-only proof-support promotion; final physical certificate still requires review of cited source fit.

proof_pipeline.scope_note: This metadata records authorship, lineage, and proof-pipeline artifact links. It does not by itself assert that the entry has a completed native analytic proof.
